_readme: Sensors register themselves - no per-sensor config needed. The collector
scans discovery.subnet, identifies each device by the sensor_id its /data
endpoint reports (SENSOR_NUMBER in firmware), and adds it automatically
under the name its firmware reports (any name works). Floor assignment
and friendly aliases are managed in the frontend: Settings > Sensors.
IPs are cached in data/known_ips.json; a full rescan runs at container
start, when a sensor stops answering, and every DISCOVERY_RESCAN_MIN
minutes (.env) - so new sensors appear on their own within that window,
or immediately after 'docker compose restart collector'.
discovery.subnet - the wifi network to scan, e.g. 192.168.0.0/24 at home.
Change this when moving to the office network.
auto_add_floor   - floor new sensors land on: Floor_7 ... Floor_10.
The sensors list below is for OPTIONAL overrides only, e.g. to pin a
or a fixed address that skips discovery:
